element_choice.justification: Almost all elements are correct: <article> for cards, <button> for actions, <address> for contact, <search> for search, native checkboxes for filters. One meaningful error: the in-stock toggle places both <label> and <input> inside <legend>, but <legend> is a caption element and should not contain form controls — the input belongs in the fieldset body.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used precisely where needed. Nav labels do not include the word 'navigation'. Add-to-cart buttons correctly use aria-labelledby to combine button text with product name IDs for disambiguation. tabindex='-1' on <main> is appropriate for skip-link focus targets. No redundant role= attributes anywhere.

heading_hierarchy.justification:
Single

('Running Shoes'), no skipped levels (h1→h2 for section/sidebar/footer headings→h3 for product names). Prices are

not headings. A code comment explicitly notes that h3 assumes a parent h2 context and should be adjusted if the component is reused elsewhere.

landmark_structure.justification: All required landmarks present and correctly scoped: <header>, labeled <nav>, <search>, <aside> (outside <main>), <main> (contains only primary content), <footer>. Three contextually appropriate skip links appear as first focusable elements (to main content, search, and filters). Every <nav> is labeled; <section> uses aria-labelledby.

form_semantics.justification: Filter form uses <fieldset>/<legend> for each group and all checkboxes have proper <label> elements. However, the in-stock fieldset incorrectly nests both <label for='in-stock'> and <input> inside <legend>; the legend element is a caption and should not contain interactive controls — the input should sit in the fieldset body.

content_realism.justification: Six distinct, realistic product names (Nike Air Zoom Pegasus 41, Adidas Ultraboost 24, Brooks Ghost 16, ASICS Gel-Kayano 31, Hoka Clifton 9, New Balance Fresh Foam X 1080v13) with varied prices and descriptive copy. No 'Product 1' or lorem ipsum anywhere.

list_semantics.justification: Navigation items, filter checkbox groups, and product cards all correctly use <ul>. Footer navigation links also use <ul> within <nav>. No lists used purely for layout; no card collection left as bare divs.

expectations_met: <main> contains only primary page content — site header, footer, and primary nav are outside it
Skip navigation links are the first focusable elements — three skip links present (content, search, filters)
Navigation uses <nav> with aria-label that does NOT include the word 'navigation'
Filter sidebar uses <form> with <fieldset>/<legend> for each filter group
Heading hierarchy flows logically: h1 for page, h2 for sections, h3 for product names
Product card heading level is documented via comment as context-dependent and adjustable
'Add to cart' buttons have accessible names distinguishing each product via aria-labelledby
Price is not marked up as a heading — uses
No redundant ARIA attributes duplicating native HTML semantics
'In stock only' toggle uses a native <input type='checkbox'>
expectations_missed: The in-stock toggle fieldset is structurally invalid: both <label for='in-stock'> and <input type='checkbox'> are placed inside the <legend> element. A <legend> is a caption for the fieldset and must not contain form controls; the checkbox and its label should be in the fieldset body, not in the legend.
additional_observations: Excellent use of aria-labelledby on Add-to-cart buttons combining button ID with product name ID — a non-obvious but correct pattern for disambiguating repeated button text.
The <search> element carries aria-label='Site search', which is good practice when multiple search landmarks could exist on a page.

The filter form has both an aria-label ('Filter by') and a visible

Filters

heading above it inside the aside; the heading is not programmatically associated with the form, creating slight redundancy, but this is a minor issue.
